name: the-design-of-everyday-things
description: Don Norman's The Design of Everyday Things — the foundational UX design toolkit covering affordances, signifiers, mapping, feedback, constraints, the seven stages of action, gulfs of execution and evaluation, human error (slips vs mistakes), and user-centered design methodology.
useCase: Understand the principles of good design — make products that are discoverable, usable, and delightful by applying Norman's foundational UX framework.
knownRisks: The book was first published in 1988 — some digital examples are dated, though the principles remain current
Norman's framework is primarily for product design, not visual/graphic design
The 'user is never wrong' philosophy can be over-applied — users can and do act irrationally
